name: HandTranscriptMd

releases.author: gabriele-cusato
releases.description:
## Bug fixes

  - Android theme change while drawing: strokes on canvas now remap colors immediately when the theme switches (light ↔ dark) — including strokes already drawn and the active pen
  color. Previously only the toolbar and background updated; the strokes stayed the wrong color.
  - Windows: modal closes on window resize — resizing the Obsidian window while the drawing overlay is open now closes it automatically and saves the SVG, preventing canvas
  coordinate corruption.

## CSS improvements (Obsidian plugin guidelines)

  - Removed all remaining !important declarations from utility classes (.hwm_hidden, .hwm_no-transition, .hwm_no-pointer) — replaced with higher-specificity compound selectors
  where needed.
  - Removed !important from pointer-events: auto on confirm and convert overlays — not needed since pointer-events is not inherited.
  - Removed !important from the native Obsidian close button suppression — replaced with a 3-class compound selector.
  - Legacy portal button changed from <button> to <div role="button"> with tabindex and keyboard support (Enter/Space) — removes dependency on Obsidian's global button style
  overrides.

releases.author: gabriele-cusato
releases.description:
## What's changed in 1.0.3
  
  ### CSS fixes

  - Removed `!important` from all rules where not strictly necessary; replaced with higher-specificity selectors
  - Fixed toolbar alignment in DrawingModal: was rendering left-aligned instead of centered
  - Fixed editor topbar background not applying inside DrawingModal (selector used `.workspace` prefix which doesn't match `.modal-container`); replaced with `.hwm_editor-view`
  prefix which covers both tab and modal contexts
  - Removed duplicate selectors (`.hwm_portal-panel`, `.hwm_color-btn`, `.hwm_clip-wrapper`)
  - Expanded all shorthand hex colors to full 6-digit format

releases.author: gabriele-cusato
releases.description:
## Fixes for Obsidian community plugin review

Resolves all ESLint errors and warnings reported by the obsidianmd validation bot on version 1.0.1.

---

### Changed

  - Added descriptions to all `eslint-disable-next-line` directives (`embed.ts`, `md-parser.ts`, `settings.ts`)
  - Removed disables of `obsidianmd/no-static-styles-assignment`: refactored runtime style assignments to use `setProperty()` / `removeProperty()` and a new `.hwm_no-transition`
  CSS class (`embed.ts`, `styles.css`)
  - Removed disables of `obsidianmd/ui/sentence-case`: moved literal UI strings to i18n keys across all 10 locale files (`settings.ts`, `locales/*.json`)
  - Replaced all `document` references with `activeDocument` (`embed.ts`, `main.ts`, `settings.ts`, `drawing-canvas.ts`, `svg-utils.ts`, `i18n.ts`)
  - Added `window.` prefix to `setTimeout`, `clearTimeout`, `requestAnimationFrame`, `cancelAnimationFrame` (`editor-view.ts`, `embed.ts`, `drawing-canvas.ts`)
  - Bumped `minAppVersion` to `1.7.2` to match actual API usage: `Vault.createFolder`, `FileManager.trashFile`, `Workspace.getLeaf`, `setActiveLeaf`, `revealLeaf`
  - Updated Gemini model from `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` to `gemini-3.1-flash-lite` (GA release — preview discontinued May 25 2026)
  - Updated `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` to `0.3.0` and added `tseslint.configs.recommendedTypeChecked` to match Obsidian validator rules

  ### Fixed

  - **Table parser**: an empty line after the first data row now correctly terminates the table block, preventing subsequent text from being absorbed as table rows
  (`md-parser.ts`)

  ### Notes

  - No user-facing feature changes except the table fix
  - Existing drawings and settings remain compatible
  - Users on Obsidian < 1.7.2 should update Obsidian before upgrading the plugin

releases.author: gabriele-cusato
releases.description:
## 1.0.1

  ### Bug fixes
  - Fixed collapse/expand animation (↕) for inline SVG previews on Windows:
    the animation now correctly clips the auto-expanded blank area, using the
    SVG's actual viewBox width instead of the canvas width setting.
  - Fixed OCR parser: empty values in comma-separated keywords are now preserved.
    `//LIST a, , c` and `//TABLE Col1, , Col3` now produce the empty entry
    instead of silently skipping it.
  - Fixed TABLE parser: each physical line is now its own table row — rows ending
    with a comma were previously merged with the next line.
  - Replaced `Vault.delete()` with `FileManager.trashFile()` to respect the
    user's file deletion preference (system trash vs Obsidian trash).
  - Resolved all ESLint errors (unsafe JSON type assignments, unused imports).
  - Moved `touch-action` from inline JS to CSS class.

releases.author: gabriele-cusato
releases.description:
## Handwriting to Markdown — v1.0.0

  Embed a handwriting canvas directly inside any Obsidian note. Draw with a stylus (or mouse), then convert your handwriting to structured Markdown using Google Gemini OCR.

  ### Features

  - **Inline canvas** — insert a drawing block anywhere in a `.md` file with a single click
  - **OCR conversion** — converts handwritten text to Markdown (headings, lists, tables, checkboxes, callouts, and more) via Google Gemini
  - **SVG storage** — drawings are saved as standard `.svg` files, visible in Obsidian even without the plugin
  - **Keyword syntax** — write `//H1`, `//LIST`, `//TABLE`, `//CHECK` etc. in your drawing to produce structured output
  - **Cross-platform** — works on Windows (modal overlay) and Android (new tab), stylus + finger scroll support
  - **No proprietary format** — `.md` files remain readable by any tool

  ### Requirements

  A free Gemini API key from [aistudio.google.com](https://aistudio.google.com) is required for OCR.
  
  ## For the SourceCode to work

```npm install``` inside the project folder to install all node_modules
